pattern: Fixing a pair of rows collapses the matrix to a 1-D array of column sums, turning the count into the classic subarray-sum-equals-target hash-map scan over every row band.
hints: For a fixed top and bottom row, a submatrix is determined by its left and right columns, so the band reduces to a single row of column sums.
Counting subarrays of that band summing to target is the prefix-sum-plus-hashmap trick used for subarray-sum-equals-k.
For every pair of rows (top, bottom), build per-column running sums then sweep left to right with a prefix-sum frequency map, adding the count of prefixes equal to current-prefix minus target.

edge_inputs.description: cancelling values give several zero submatrices
single cell that is not the target
negative target reachable only by a band
row matrix with repeated prefix sums

stress.description: moderate matrix over a small value range so many bands hit the target
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 24)))
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 24)))
    matrix = [[rng.randint(-3, 3) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]
    target = rng.randint(-5, 5)
    return [matrix, target]
stress.note: small magnitudes concentrate sums so the target is frequently hit
